question:
What makes the "slippery slope" argument a logical fallacy in many debates?
A) It assumes that a relatively small first step will inevitably lead to an extreme and disastrous chain of events without evidence.
B) It implies that ice is dangerous to walk on.
C) It relies entirely on mathematical formulas to win an argument.
D) It demands immediate physical action from the opponent.
